topics.name: Basic Economic Concepts and Production
topics.description: Scarcity, choice, scale of preference, opportunity cost, production, division of labor, and business units.

subtopics.name: Fundamental Economic Concepts
objectives: Explain basic economic concepts: wants, scarcity, choice, scale of preference, opportunity cost

subtopics.name: Production and Factors of Production
objectives: Describe primary, secondary, and tertiary production stages
Analyze land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship as factors of production and their rewards
Explain division of labor, specialization, and localization of industries

subtopics.name: Business Organizations
objectives: Compare sole proprietorships, partnerships, joint-stock companies, co-operatives, and public enterprises

topics.name: Microeconomics: Demand, Supply, and Markets
topics.description: Laws of demand and supply, price equilibrium, elasticity, consumer behavior, and market structures.

subtopics.name: Demand and Supply
objectives: State laws of demand and supply and construct demand and supply schedules/curves
Calculate equilibrium price and equilibrium quantity algebraically and graphically
Distinguish changes in demand/supply vs. changes in quantity demanded/supplied

subtopics.name: Elasticity of Demand and Supply
objectives: Calculate Price Elasticity of Demand (PED), Income Elasticity (YED), and Cross Elasticity (XED)
Calculate Price Elasticity of Supply (PES) and explain factors affecting elasticity

subtopics.name: Market Structures
objectives: Compare perfect competition, monopoly, monopolistic competition, and oligopoly price/output determination

topics.name: Macroeconomics, Money, and Public Finance
topics.description: National income accounting, money, banking, inflation, public finance, taxation, and fiscal policy.

subtopics.name: National Income Accounting
objectives: Define Gross Domestic Product (GDP), Gross National Product (GNP), Net National Product (NNP), and Per Capita Income
Calculate national income using Output, Income, and Expenditure methods

subtopics.name: Money, Banking, and Inflation
objectives: Explain functions of money, commercial banks, and central bank monetary policy tools
Analyze causes, effects, and control of inflation and deflation

subtopics.name: Public Finance and Taxation
objectives: Explain government revenue sources, recurrent/capital expenditure, and budget types (balanced, surplus, deficit)
Distinguish direct taxation and indirect taxation and principles of taxation (canons of taxation)
